finding: A shell comment line beginning with # inside a fenced code block is parsed as a markdown heading by the task import parser. It resets the task declaration context, so a well-formed card with valid front matter fails with ATM_TASKS_PLAN_EMPTY even though the parser separately reports having detected the correct heading. The emitted diagnostic is also misleading: it reports ATM_TASK_IMPORT_REFERENCE_ONLY_ID_FRAGMENT naming the card id at a source line where that id does not appear.
expectedBehavior: Fence state must be tracked before heading detection: content inside a fenced block is never a heading and must not affect task declaration context. Diagnostics must cite a line that actually contains the referenced token.
evidenceOrRepro: Resolved by ATM-GOV-0232 evidence: node --strip-types tests/cli/task-import-diagnostic-contract.test.ts covers backtick and tilde fenced shell comments, verifies no task import is created, and verifies diagnostics cite the fenced triggering source line. node --strip-types tests/cli/task-import-canonical-id-boundary.test.ts covers canonical id right-boundary fragments and fenced markdown/yaml examples.
followUp: No standalone follow-up remains for the fence-before-heading parser boundary. Keep future parser work generic and do not special-case shell comments.
